appeal_letter:
Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for Semaglutide (Wegovy) as prescribed by the treating provider. The denial states that the request does not meet Cigna's coverage criteria, that the employer-sponsored pharmacy benefit plan has a strict exclusion for all weight-loss medications, and that the member does not have a diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes, which would be required for coverage of similar GLP-1 medications under the medical benefit. On those grounds the request was denied as a plan exclusion and as not medically necessary.

I respectfully ask the plan to reconsider this determination in light of the clinical purpose of the prescription. According to the clinical context, the patient is on the verge of developing Type 2 Diabetes, and the treating provider prescribed Wegovy to prevent the onset of diabetes rather than for cosmetic weight loss. The basis of this appeal is that the medication has been prescribed for a preventive medical purpose identified by the treating provider, and I ask that the request be evaluated in that light rather than solely as a weight-loss treatment.

I recognize that the denial cites an employer plan exclusion for weight-loss medications. I ask the plan to confirm whether the prescription, given its stated preventive purpose, falls within the scope of that exclusion, and to identify the specific plan language being applied. I also ask the plan to identify what clinical documentation would be required to support coverage on the basis of medical necessity for diabetes prevention.

I request that Cigna overturn the denial and approve coverage for Wegovy as prescribed. In the alternative, I request a written explanation of the exact plan-exclusion language relied upon and a description of the documentation the plan would require to establish medical necessity for the stated preventive purpose.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Thank you for your reconsideration of this matter.

Sincerely,
The Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Treating provider's letter of medical necessity describing the preventive purpose of the prescription and the risk of progression to Type 2 Diabetes
Laboratory results supporting prediabetes or elevated diabetes risk (for example A1c or fasting glucose values)
Documentation of the patient's weight history and any prior interventions attempted
The full plan document language defining the weight-loss medication exclusion and any exceptions
Records of any other treatments or medications tried to address the underlying condition
Clinical notes describing the provider's rationale for selecting Wegovy for diabetes prevention
